title: Schedonorus montanus
taxonomy: Schedonorus montanus is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Schedonorus, and its full classification is: Schedonorus montanus, Schedonorus (in some classifications this is spelled Festuca), Poaceae, Magnoliophyta, Plantae. This classification indicates that it is a type of grass. The Poaceae family is a large group of monocotyledonous flowering plants, also known as true grasses. Schedonorus montanus is a synonym for Festuca montana.

intro: Schedonorus montanus, also known as mountain timothy grass, is a perennial grass species native to North America. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory properties, antioxidant activity, and potential to alleviate respiratory issues such as asthma.
